question: Based on Table 5.3-9/10 data and the text discussion of Charpy requirements, do the NuScale RPV materials maintain adequate fracture toughness through end of life?

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, in compliance with technical specifications and based on as-built material properties. It also details the evaluation of pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy, confirming that predicted values remain within regulatory limits for the NuScale reactor’s 60-year design life.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, housing critical reactor systems and providing structural support, while its materials and fabrication comply with stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code requirements to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) comply with relevant U.S. nuclear regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria, by designing, fabricating, and testing reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, Class 1 standards. It also clarifies the classification of connected piping and references the applicable ASME BPVC edition and regulatory guides used for the design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides figures and tables with the required data to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, providing a critical barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, and operational requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards for quality assurance and material integrity. It also lists the applicable codes and standards used for construction, handling, and surveillance of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the information for regulatory purposes, and any copies must include the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, detailing its structure and topics.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards related to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component design and inspection.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, specifying the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components must comply with the latest ASME BPVC Section XI requirements, as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a, with inspection intervals and code editions clearly defined in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat from the reactor after shutdown, especially when normal cooling is unavailable, ensuring safe shutdown without external power. It maintains the reactor coolant system (RCS) temperature below 420°F within 36 hours, protecting fuel and system integrity, and its reliability and risk significance are rigorously evaluated. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), with both systems able to function together if needed.

edges.description: Section A specifies the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded for the RPV, which are necessary to maintain the integrity of the RPV described in Section B as a key pressure-retaining component.

Section A describes the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which supports Section B's claim that the reactor vessel and components comply with ASME Code and regulatory requirements for integrity.

Section A describes the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with pressure-temperature limits, which are based on material properties such as Charpy upper-shelf energy discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the requirement to develop operating procedures that ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which is necessary to maintain the integrity of the RPV materials described in Section B.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, while Section B outlines inspection and handling requirements to maintain the RPV's condition; both sections are connected by their roles in ensuring the RPV's structural integrity and compliance with regulatory requirements.

Section A describes the physical design and function of the NuScale RPV, while Section B states that the RPV is designed and constructed in compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, connecting the vessel's design to its regulatory adequacy.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale RPV and its role in the reactor system, while Section B specifies the regulatory and code requirements (including Charpy test requirements) that the RPV materials must meet, thus connecting the vessel's design to its required fracture toughness performance.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies the inspection, shipment, and installation requirements that apply to the RPV as described in Section A.

Section A states that the RPV meets ASME BPVC requirements as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a, while Section B discusses specific compliance with ASME BPVC provisions for welds and attachments, indicating that the detailed practices in B are part of the broader compliance claimed in A.

Section A states that the NuScale RPV meets ASME Code requirements as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a, while Section B describes the physical components and function of the RPV, connecting the regulatory compliance in A to the specific materials and structure discussed in B.

Section A states that the RPV and associated components comply with ASME BPVC Section III requirements, while Section B specifies that shipment, handling, and storage of the RPV are performed according to the same ASME BPVC Section III and related standards, thus Section B provides detailed implementation of the general compliance described in Section A.

Section A discusses the analyses and data (including Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy) used to evaluate the RPV's compliance with fracture toughness requirements, while Section B describes the materials and structural context of the RPV to which those requirements and analyses apply.

Section A discusses compliance with specific ASME BPVC requirements for welds and attachments on the RPV, while Section B outlines the broader inspection and handling requirements for the RPV as per ASME BPVC and related standards, with Section A providing detailed specifications that fall under the general requirements described in Section B.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale RPV materials and their function, while Section B references the inspection and handling requirements that ensure the RPV materials' integrity, which is relevant to assessing whether fracture toughness is maintained.

Section A is the preface providing document context, while Section B specifies that the RPV materials comply with regulatory codes (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC), establishing the regulatory framework for evaluating fracture toughness requirements discussed elsewhere.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical and regulatory basis for reactor pressure vessel (RPV) material integrity, while Section B discusses the implementation of operating procedures to ensure compliance with pressure-temperature limits, both of which are necessary to demonstrate that the RPV materials maintain adequate fracture toughness through end of life.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that are cited in Section B's description of the reactor vessel, establishing the regulatory and technical basis for the RPV's design and material requirements.
Section A lists references, including ASME and EPRI documents, that are cited to support the claims in Section B regarding compliance with regulatory codes and standards for RPV materials.

Section A describes the material selection and standards compliance for RPV threaded fasteners, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, both of which are connected by their roles in maintaining the integrity and safe operation of the reactor pressure vessel under regulatory requirements.

Section A provides specific material and inspection requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, while Section B gives a general description of the RPV and its role in the reactor coolant system, with Section A specifying details for the components described in Section B.

Section A describes the material selection and ASME BPVC compliance for RPV threaded fasteners, while Section B states that the entire RPV and pressure boundary components comply with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC requirements, thus Section A provides specific details supporting the general compliance claim in Section B.

Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which is necessary to maintain the vessel's fracture toughness as discussed in the analysis question, while Section A describes the Decay Heat Removal System that supports safe shutdown and helps prevent conditions that could challenge those limits.

Section A describes the Decay Heat Removal System's role in maintaining safe reactor conditions, which indirectly affects the operational environment and thus the fracture toughness requirements for the RPV materials discussed in Section B.

Section A lists references that establish the regulatory and technical standards for material selection and quality assurance, while Section B describes how the threaded fastener materials used in the RPV conform to these referenced standards, specifically ASME BPVC requirements.
